QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_13

question.text: An existing merchant banker wants to conduct non-SEBI regulated activities after the Amendment Regulations 2025. Within what time frame must it transfer such activities to Strategic Business Units (SBUs)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Existing MBs must transfer their non-SEBI regulated activities to Strategic Business Units (SBUs) within 6 months of the Amendment Regulations 2025 coming into effect. SBUs ensure proper segregation and ring-fencing of these activities. **The Trap:** 12 months might seem like a reasonable transition period, but the regulation is more stringent at 6 months to ensure swift compliance with the new framework. **Cross-Exam Context:** Transition timelines for regulatory changes are commonly tested — NISM Series V-A tests similar transition periods for mutual fund distributor compliance. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Start SBU setup immediately upon notification — 6 months passes quickly when you factor in legal structuring, compliance setup, and operational readiness.
